subject: english
learning_objectives: Define an informal letter and identify to whom it is written
List the structural features of an informal letter
Write a well-structured informal letter on a given topic

sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: Informal letters are private or personal letters written to people who are very familiar to us: parents, relatives, friends, classmates, and others with whom we have a close relationship.

sections.heading: Features of an Informal Letter
sections.content:
1. Writer's address (top right or left of the page)

2. Date

3. Salutation – use the recipient's first name:
   Dear Peter, | Dear Chike, | Dear Kemi, | Dear Father
   When 'My' is included, 'd' is lowercase: My dear Mother

4. Introduction – begin by acknowledging receipt of a letter, asking after the person's health, or any other friendly opener.

5. Body of the letter – do exactly what the question instructs.

6. Subscript (closing):
   Yours sincerely, | Yours affectionately, | Your loving daughter, | With love from
   Written at the bottom right. No signature required – write your first name and full stop:
     Yours sincerely,
     Audu.

     Yours ever,
     Lizzy
